sidebar.brand.title: Open Home
sidebar.user.title: About you
sidebar.toggle.title: Toggle sidebar
home.state.title: State of the mirror
home.release.title: Latest from the mirror
home.continue.title: Continue
me.preferences.title: Preferences
me.preferences.currencyNote: Applies to the Context Rail and the budget dashboard.
me.preferences.empty: No preferences to set yet. More settings arrive with future stories — language, timezone, theme.
me.stats.title: How the mirror sees you
me.data.title: Data
me.error.tooLong: Name must be 40 characters or fewer.
me.saved.name: Name updated
me.preferences.languageNote: Sets the language for the mirror's interface. Conversations themselves follow your message — the mirror replies in whatever language you write to it.
conversation.confirmDelete: Delete this exchange? The user message and its reply will be removed from this conversation.
conversation.lab.bypassTitle: Bypass persona routing — responds with soul+ego only (Identity Lab exploration)
composed.title: Composed prompt
header.cast.label: Cast
header.cast.turnsZero: no turns yet in this session
header.context.label: Context
header.mode.label: Mode
header.menu.forgetConfirm: Forget this conversation? This cannot be undone.
conversations.intro: Every conversation you've had, across personas and scopes. Filter to narrow. Click any row to continue from where it stopped.
scope.summary.empty: Nothing to summarize yet — add briefing or situation first.
scope.summary.timeout: Summary generation timed out. Try again in a moment.
scope.summary.failed: Summary generation failed. Check server logs for details.
scope.sessions.title: Conversations
scope.sessions.emptyOrg: This organization has no conversations tagged to it yet. Start a new one from the conversation page, or tag an existing session via the Context Rail.
scope.sessions.emptyJourney: This journey has no conversations tagged to it yet. Start a new one from the conversation page, or tag an existing session via the Context Rail.
scope.band.concludedHint: Finished cycles. Out of the sidebar, but still routable when reception sees a question about their territory.
scope.archived.heading: Archived
scope.create.keyTitle: lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only
scope.create.keyHint: stable identifier — letters, numbers, hyphens; set once
scope.workshop.summarySub: used by reception routing and by the scope card · regenerated automatically on Save
scope.workshop.summaryEmpty: No summary yet. It will be generated on the next Save, or you can regenerate manually below.
scope.lifecycle.heading: Lifecycle
organizations.intro: Broader situational scopes you're in — a venture, a community, a role. Each organization can contain journeys. When reception detects one, its briefing and current situation join the composed prompt.
organizations.create.heading: New organization
organizations.workshop.headerHelpPart2: is who this organization is — stable.
organizations.workshop.headerHelpPart3: is where it stands right now — evolves over weeks and months. Both enter the composed prompt when reception detects this organization.
organizations.workshop.briefingHint: Who this organization is. Purpose, mission, posture, values. Stable — edited rarely.
organizations.workshop.situationHint: Where this organization stands right now. Current phase, active initiatives, what's in play. Evolving — edited as state changes.
organizations.lifecycle.markConcludedNot: Finished its cycle. Leaves the sidebar and the default list noise, but stays routable and visible in the concluded band on /organizations.
organizations.lifecycle.archiveActiveNot: Out of routing and out of the default list. Readable via direct URL, revealed via "Show archived".
organizations.lifecycle.reopenNote: Back to active. The organization returns to the sidebar and resumes as a frontline scope.
organizations.lifecycle.archiveConcluded: Skip the active state — take this concluded org out of the default list and routing entirely.
organizations.lifecycle.unarchiveNote: Restore to active. The organization becomes eligible for routing again.
organizations.lifecycle.deleteConfirm: Delete organization "{name}"? Journeys linked to it become personal (organization unlinks, journeys survive). This cannot be undone.
journeys.intro: Narrower situational scopes — a pursuit, a period, a crossing you're going through. A journey may belong to an organization or stand alone as personal. When reception detects one, its briefing and current situation join the composed prompt.
journeys.create.heading: New journey
journeys.create.organizationHint: leave blank for a personal journey; pick an organization to group it
journeys.workshop.organizationHint: Leave blank for a personal journey; pick an organization to group it there.
journeys.workshop.headerHelpPart2: is what this journey is — what you're pursuing or crossing. Stable.
journeys.workshop.headerHelpPart3: is where it stands right now — the active phase or focus. Evolves. Both enter the composed prompt when reception detects this journey.
journeys.workshop.briefingHint: What this journey is. Purpose, what you're pursuing, what makes it a crossing. Stable — edited rarely.
journeys.workshop.situationHint: Where this journey stands right now. Active phase, focus, progress. Evolving — edited as state changes.
journeys.lifecycle.markConcludedNote: Finished its cycle. Leaves the sidebar and the default list noise, but stays routable and visible in the concluded band on /journeys.
journeys.lifecycle.archiveActiveNote: Out of routing and out of the default list. Readable via direct URL, revealed via "Show archived".
journeys.lifecycle.reopenNote: Back to active. The journey returns to the sidebar and resumes as a frontline scope.
journeys.lifecycle.archiveConcludedNote: Skip the active state — take this concluded journey out of the default list and routing entirely.
journeys.lifecycle.unarchiveNote: Restore to active. The journey becomes eligible for routing again.
journeys.lifecycle.deleteConfirm: Delete journey "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
personas.intro: Specialized lenses the mirror activates for specific domains. Reception picks one when the message clearly lands in a persona's territory; otherwise the base voice answers. Each persona's content joins the composed prompt when it's active.
personas.empty: No personas yet. Create them from the Cognitive Map.
layer.help.self.soul: Deep identity, frequency, nature. What you are before you are anything specific.
layer.help.ego.identity: Operational identity — who you are in the day-to-day, what you do, how you introduce yourself.
layer.help.ego.expression: How you speak. Format, vocabulary, punctuation, style. Separated from behavior so problems of form and problems of method can be diagnosed independently.
layer.help.ego.behavior: Conduct, posture, method. What you do and how you position yourself when you act.
layer.help.persona: A specialized lens the mirror activates when reception detects the persona's domain. The content below joins the composed prompt in place of (or alongside) the base voice.
layer.composedTitle: View the full composed system prompt
layer.color.label: Color
layer.color.sub: shown on avatars, bubbles, and badges throughout the system
layer.summarySub: shown on Psyche Map cards and used by reception routing · regenerated automatically on Save
layer.readEmpty: Nothing written yet. Click Edit to start.
map.invitation.soul: Your soul is the deepest voice — what you are before you are anything specific. Frequency, nature, origin. Open the workshop to write your foundation.
map.invitation.identity: Your operational identity — how you show up in the day-to-day. What you do, what you're known for, how you introduce yourself. Open the workshop to set it.
map.invitation.expression: How you speak — format, vocabulary, punctuation, style. Separated from behavior so problems of form and problems of method can be diagnosed independently. Open the workshop to define your expression.
map.invitation.behavior: Your behavior — conduct, posture, method. What you do and how you position yourself when you act. Open the workshop to define them.
map.invitation.personasPart1: Personas are the specialized voices the mirror speaks in — a mentor who listens with care, a strategist who cuts through noise, a writer who crafts with precision. Each persona is a lens the ego activates when a particular kind of depth is needed. Click
map.persona.nameHint: lowercase, hyphens allowed — used as the persona's key
map.persona.deleteConfirm: Delete this persona? This cannot be undone.
map.memory.heading: Memory
map.memory.subtitle: what flows through
map.memory.attention.label: Attention
map.memory.conversations.label: Conversations
map.memory.insights.label: Insights
